commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Judge, or "rule "(Judges viii. 22.) as Christ does over all. (Calmet)
Plough-shares. Hebrew, "scythes "(Haydock) or "coulters. "(Worthington) (Mart. xiv. 34.) (Calmet)
Learn The law of Christ is a law of peace; and all his true subjects, as much as lies in them, love and keep peace with all the world. (Challoner)
They will sustain injuries meekly, 1 Corinthians vi. (Worthington)
When Christ appeared, the Roman empire enjoyed peace. (Calmet)
Aspera tum positis mite scent sæcula bellis (Virgil, Æneid i.)

commentaries.author: Richard Challoner
commentaries.content: Neither shall they learn: The law of Christ is a law of peace; and all his true subjects, as much as lies in them love and keep peace with all the world.
